title: Investigative Journalism

jokes.text: You could even enroll in community college - Right now it sounds as boring as real life, But it is not.
jokes.setup: Characters describing exciting video game activities
jokes.punchline: Enrolling in community college is presented as exciting gameplay when they're literally at community college
jokes.explanation: Classic Community meta-humor - characters unknowingly describing their own situation as boring while claiming it's exciting in a game

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Dude, we could be doing quests, unlocking achievements, finding hidden treasure...

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We're literally at community college right now.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: Amsterdam, I think. I'll know more when I find my camera.
jokes.setup: Asked where she went on vacation
jokes.punchline: Implies she was so intoxicated/wild she doesn't remember and needs photographic evidence
jokes.explanation: Economical character-specific humor that establishes Britta's wild vacation without being explicit

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I don't know, I blacked out. Do you have pictures?

jokes.text: These ironic tee shirts are all the rage.
jokes.setup: Pierce claiming he became even cooler
jokes.punchline: Pierce fundamentally misunderstands ironic t-shirts by being earnest about them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce character moment - trying to be cool but missing the point entirely

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I became even cooler. I got an ironic t-shirt.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: It says 'World's Best Dad' but I'm not a dad. Get it?

jokes.text: I wish I was still a lawyer. I'm only here because I'm hot for britta, And I don't want to be anybody's friend.
jokes.setup: Jeff making a serious confession about his motivations
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest admission that undermines the group dynamic
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff moment of devastating honesty that creates awkward tension

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, I need to be honest with all of you. Everything I do here, every study group meeting, every adventure we go on... it's all just because I'm trying to get into Britta's pants.

jokes.text: Just kidding. Bring it in here, ya knuckleheads!
jokes.setup: Jeff's brutal honesty from previous moment
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal into forced cheerfulness
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that establishes Jeff's new fake-positive persona

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I was just being honest.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: But hey, who wants pizza?

jokes.text: He kept his upbeat humor and charm Even in the 11th year of the korean war.
jokes.setup: Comparing Jeff to Hawkeye from M.A.S.H.
jokes.punchline: Korean War only lasted 3 years - Abed references the show running longer than the actual war
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about TV shows outlasting their source material, very Abed-specific observation

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The Korean War only lasted 3 years. M.A.S.H. ran for 11 seasons.

jokes.text: Did you just teleport here?
jokes.setup: Buddy suddenly appears in the study room
jokes.punchline: Jeff's incredulous reaction to Buddy's sudden appearance
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction to establish Buddy's creepy ability to appear suddenly

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Buddy? What are you doing here?

jokes.text: No, I snuck in during the group hug.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking how Buddy appeared
jokes.punchline: Buddy infiltrated their intimate moment
jokes.explanation: Establishes Buddy as uncomfortably intrusive while being oddly logical

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: How did you even get in here?

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: I infiltrated your intimate moment.

jokes.text: I've never seen you before in my life.
jokes.setup: Buddy asking if they remember him from Spanish class
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt denial despite Buddy clearly knowing them
jokes.explanation: Brutally direct rejection that creates uncomfortable tension

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: You guys remember me from Spanish class?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: He sits in the corner next to the dead plant.
jokes.setup: Abed remembering Buddy from class
jokes.punchline: Specific visual detail of dead plant makes the corner sound extra pathetic
jokes.explanation: Abed's observational skills used to paint a sad picture of Buddy's usual spot

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, Buddy from class. I remember him now.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: There's a dead plant in that corner that makes the whole thing sound extra pathetic.

jokes.text: Sometimes I have a tendency to melt into the background. It's just my body type.
jokes.setup: Buddy explaining why people don't notice him
jokes.punchline: Blames his invisibility on his 'body type' rather than personality
jokes.explanation: Buddy's misguided self-awareness - blames physical rather than behavioral reasons for being forgettable

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: People don't notice me because of my body type, not my personality.

jokes.text: Britta cheated? But why?
jokes.setup: Buddy recounting events where someone got an F
jokes.punchline: Annie's shock that Britta would cheat implies she has higher standards
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent reaction that plays on Annie's academic integrity

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: Yeah, and then Britta got an F on the exam.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Britta cheated?! I can't believe she would cheat!

jokes.text: Gimme back my bra, annie. - I'm not even wearing a bra. - Oh!
jokes.setup: Buddy recounting random memory fragments
jokes.punchline: Bizarre non-sequitur about bra exchange with awkward revelation
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist moment that creates uncomfortable awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: So there was this time Britta lent Annie her bra, right? And Annie's like 'This doesn't fit,' and Britta goes 'Well it fits me fine,' and then Annie says 'Yeah, because you stuffed it with socks!'

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I did not stuff it with socks!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: There were literally five pairs of gym socks in there.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Why are we talking about this?

jokes.text: I sleep a lot in class. That one might have been a dream.
jokes.setup: After recounting the bra incident
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that undermines all his previous 'memories'
jokes.explanation: Good payoff that retroactively makes his weird memories even funnier

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: I don't actually remember any of that. I just wanted to impress you guys.

jokes.text: ♪ I can't count the reasons I should stay ♪
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to give a diplomatic rejection
jokes.punchline: Buddy breaks into song mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Cringe musical moment that shows Buddy's desperate need to be liked

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Buddy, I appreciate your enthusiasm, but I don't think this is going to work out.

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: ♪ Why does it have to be this way, why can't we just stay together, why does love always have to... ♪

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Are you... are you singing right now?

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: ♪ Fly away from me! ♪

jokes.text: ♪ spanish studyin' is better ♪ ♪ when you're buddyin' ♪ ♪ around, yeah ♪
jokes.setup: Buddy continuing to be overly enthusiastic
jokes.punchline: Forced rhyme using his own name
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that's intentionally bad to show Buddy's desperation

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: I'm so excited I could burst! This is the best day ever, and you know what? That rhymes with my name — Buddy, studdy, mud-ee! Get it? I'm just so thrilled to be here, I could buddy-buddy-pal around all day long!

jokes.text: Sorry about the ruse, but I have something better Than exfoliating soap to rub on you.
jokes.setup: Dean admitting to fake text about Sephora samples
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate double entendre about rubbing things on Jeff
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean inappropriate sexual undertone delivered obliviously

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I may have exaggerated about those Sephora samples.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: But I'd like to rub some things on Jeff.

jokes.text: This is the first desk I've seen in six months that doesn't have 'zeppelin rules' carved into it.
jokes.setup: Dean showing Jeff the newspaper office
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about the quality of Greendale facilities
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail that establishes Greendale's low standards while being quotable

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Welcome to the newspaper office, Jeff. State-of-the-art facilities.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Dean, this is a broom closet with a typewriter.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: A very nice broom closet.

jokes.text: Pizza wars--who's got the best slice in town? Start with big nick's. Bring us a large, with sausage.
jokes.setup: Jeff assigning important journalism work
jokes.punchline: The 'investigation' is just getting them free pizza
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff manipulation disguised as legitimate work assignment

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Alright everyone, I'm assigning you all to work on an important investigative journalism piece. This is serious work that could expose real wrongdoing.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: The investigation is into which local pizza place gives the biggest slices.

jokes.text: Find out how much they'll sell you Of anything cold and imported.
jokes.setup: Jeff assigning undercover investigation of underage liquor sales
jokes.punchline: Thinly veiled request for free alcohol
jokes.explanation: Jeff using his position to get personal benefits while maintaining plausible deniability

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I need someone to go undercover and investigate illegal underage liquor sales at the local convenience store.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: That sounds dangerous. I'll do it.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Actually, I was thinking someone who looks younger. Troy?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm not trying to get arrested.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It's for the greater good. And if it works out, you can keep whatever alcohol you confiscate.

jokes.text: Are you a reporter?
jokes.setup: Annie questioning Jeff's ethics
jokes.punchline: Jeff deflects with fake journalistic authority
jokes.explanation: Jeff's manipulative deflection technique in action

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: How can you just make stuff up like that?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not making it up. I read it somewhere. Probably.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's not how facts work!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, Annie, Annie. I've been doing this a long time. You develop a sense for what's true. It's called journalistic integrity.

jokes.text: And because I love crosswords.
jokes.setup: Annie complaining about being assigned crosswords because she's a girl
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits she actually does love them
jokes.explanation: Annie undermining her own feminist complaint with honest admission

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: This is so sexist! Just because I'm a girl, they assume I love crosswords.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I mean, I do love them, but that's not the point!

jokes.text: Ace? You can do that?
jokes.setup: Jeff calling Annie his 'ace news hound'
jokes.punchline: Annie's surprised delight at being given a cool nickname
jokes.explanation: Annie's earnest excitement at being treated like a real reporter

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You're my ace news hound.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Really? I'm your ace news hound?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's right.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I've never had a cool nickname before!

jokes.text: the news of senor chang's death Over winter break was a shock to us all. But we should take comfort in the fact That he did not suffer When his moped hit the side of that arby's.
jokes.setup: Announcing teacher's death solemnly
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous cause of death involving moped and Arby's
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist dark humor with specific mundane details making it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I have to inform you all that Professor has passed away.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: He was hit by a moped in the Arby's parking lot while trying to retrieve a curly fry that had blown out of his bag.

jokes.text: So sad. But best semester ever.
jokes.setup: Solemn moment about Chang's death
jokes.punchline: Troy's immediate celebration of no longer having Chang as teacher
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - inappropriate honesty delivered with innocent enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Chang is dead. We're free.

jokes.text: That was a professional actress. I am a man who can never die.
jokes.setup: Students believing Chang died over break
jokes.punchline: Chang's dramatic entrance claiming immortality
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with Chang's characteristic megalomania - great setup/payoff

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You fools! Did you really think a mere break could end me? I am immortal! I have walked this earth for centuries, disguised as a security guard at a community college!

jokes.text: We've got three chicks, three dudes, And now two weirdos. Sorry, abed.
jokes.setup: Troy analyzing group demographics
jokes.punchline: Casually categorizes Abed as a weirdo then apologizes
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent bluntness about Abed being weird, with polite afterthought apology

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So we've got the smart one, the hot one, the old one... and Abed, who's just a total weirdo.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Hey!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Sorry, sorry — the weird one. I apologize.

jokes.text: Or he could have a row of jars waiting for our genitals.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether Buddy could be dangerous
jokes.punchline: Pierce's specific and disturbing serial killer imagery
jokes.explanation: Memorably dark and specific Pierce paranoia - disturbing but funny

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You think Buddy could be dangerous? I've thought about this. If Buddy were a serial killer, he'd use a wood chipper. He'd be very specific about it - he'd probably have a whole system. He'd keep detailed notes in a binder, organized by victim profile. The binder would be color-coded.

jokes.text: Yo, I need my genitals.
jokes.setup: Pierce's disturbing genital jar comment
jokes.punchline: Troy's earnest practical concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy reaction - takes Pierce's weird comment at face value with innocent concern

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. So you're just keeping them... in a jar? Like, how often do you check on them? Because if you're not rotating them, they're gonna get all stuck together.

jokes.text: 'cause you're hilarious!
jokes.setup: Shirley suggesting she could also just have fun and crack wise
jokes.punchline: Pierce's sarcastic response to Shirley's comedic abilities
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm that plays on Shirley not being the funny one in the group

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I could be a astronaut, but we both know that ship has sailed.

jokes.text: And one french kid named lebron. That's profiling.
jokes.setup: Text sent to black students about concert time change
jokes.punchline: Random French student named LeBron also got the text
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail that makes the profiling accusation more complex and funny

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: A text was sent to all the black students about the concert time change.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: But somehow a random French student named LeBron also got the text.

jokes.text: Well...Nobody's that stupid, right?
jokes.setup: Annie suggesting calling the number that sent the text
jokes.punchline: Assumes no one would be dumb enough to answer
jokes.explanation: Sets up expectation that someone will indeed be that stupid

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: We should call the number that sent the text.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Why would we do that?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: To find out who sent it.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, no one's dumb enough to answer their own trap call.

jokes.text: Hello? Um... wrong number.
jokes.setup: Annie calling the suspicious number
jokes.punchline: Dean immediately answers and gives worst possible cover story
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Annie's setup - Dean's immediate panicked lie

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Hello?

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Oh hey Annie! Yeah, I'm just at home... doing normal Dean things. Definitely not in the bushes outside your apartment. Why would you even think that? I'm wearing normal clothes right now, not all black or anything. And this isn't a walkie-talkie, it's a regular phone. A regular phone that I definitely didn't buy at a spy store.

jokes.text: No, that-- that was a good cover.
jokes.setup: Dean's obvious 'wrong number' lie
jokes.punchline: Dean trying to convince himself it was believable
jokes.explanation: Dean's self-delusion about his terrible lying abilities

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Wrong number! Yeah, that's what happened. I dialed wrong. It happens to everyone.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: A very believable mistake. Very natural. Nobody would question that.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: I mean, who hasn't accidentally called someone and then immediately hung up without saying anything? That's totally normal behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Dean
dialogue.line: Yep. Nailed it. That was my best lie yet.

jokes.text: Thank you guys very much-- much--real much? Duh!
jokes.setup: Buddy trying to give heartfelt thanks
jokes.punchline: Stumbles over simple phrase and calls himself dumb
jokes.explanation: Buddy's nervous stumbling over words shows his desperation to fit in

jokes.text: Conjugate. Conjugal visit!
jokes.setup: Spanish lesson about conjugating verbs
jokes.punchline: Buddy makes terrible prison joke connection
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad wordplay showing Buddy's desperate attempts at humor

jokes.text: ♪ annie got an essay portion ♪ ♪ annie gonna move her luscious fanny ♪ ♪ all day long ♪
jokes.setup: Spanish lesson continuing
jokes.punchline: Buddy's inappropriate sexual song about Annie
jokes.explanation: Cringey inappropriate behavior that makes everyone uncomfortable

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: Ay, ay, ay, Annie, you're so fine, so fine, so fine. Ay, ay, ay, Annie, you're on my mind, my mind, my mind. I want to take you to the fiesta, show you my best-a, if you know what I mean, ay, ay, ay!

jokes.text: Annie's pretty young. We try not to sexualize her.
jokes.setup: Buddy's inappropriate song about Annie
jokes.punchline: Jeff's direct-to-camera meta commentary about the show's approach
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Jeff addresses both Buddy and the audience about the show's self-awareness - became iconic

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, so that happened. And before you ask, no, we're not going to address it. We're just going to move on like it never happened. Because that's what we do here.

jokes.text: I was thinking, as a group, We could start saying, 'you go, girl.' Not in a way that's, you know, 'oh, I'm so cool saying you go, girl.' But in a way that we're kinda winking at it.
jokes.setup: Buddy suggesting improvements to the group
jokes.punchline: Overly complicated explanation of ironic catchphrase usage
jokes.explanation: Buddy's desperate attempt to contribute something cool but over-explaining ruins it

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: You know what this group needs? A more sophisticated approach to ironic catchphrase deployment. See, what you're doing is you're using phrases that are inherently contradictory to their surface meaning, but you're not accounting for the meta-textual layer of self-awareness that makes the irony actually function. You need to establish a baseline of sincerity first, then subvert it with calculated insincerity, while simultaneously maintaining just enough earnestness that observers can't tell if you're being genuine or not. That's the sweet spot.

jokes.text: Did anyone see paul blart: Mall co-- I'm like that, okay? I'm fat and I'm... Physical, okay? And I'm strong. Hmm? And I can kick.
jokes.setup: Buddy trying to sell himself to the group
jokes.punchline: Awkward self-description cutting off movie reference
jokes.explanation: Buddy's desperate pitch becomes more uncomfortable as he lists his physical attributes

jokes.text: [Buddy kicks Jeff in face, giving him nosebleed]
jokes.setup: Buddy claiming he can kick
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates by accidentally kicking Jeff
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical payoff to Buddy's boasting - immediate consequences

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: I can kick.

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: Watch this.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Ow!

jokes.text: I get 'em when it's dry And when my face gets kicked.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining his nosebleed
jokes.punchline: Casual addition of face-kicking as normal cause
jokes.explanation: Jeff's dry delivery makes the absurd situation sound routine

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I got a nosebleed from getting kicked in the face.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: It happens.

jokes.text: Your friend Gary is so boring. He was raised in Finland. He doesn't get our humor.
jokes.setup: Shirley suggesting her friend Gary
jokes.punchline: Pierce's xenophobic dismissal based on nationality
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce prejudice that shows his casual bigotry. Finland is a perfectly random target.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: My friend Gary is really nice. You should meet him.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Gary? What kind of name is that? Sounds foreign.

jokes.text: I think it's obvious to everyone That this buddy fellow has a few froot loops loose.
jokes.setup: Group meeting to discuss Buddy
jokes.punchline: Pierce's unique take on 'screws loose' saying
jokes.explanation: Pierce's unique phrasing shows his old-fashioned perspective

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know what they say - he's got a few screws loose. In my day, we just called it being a Jew.

jokes.text: I can't stop thinking about his high kick. His ratio of girth to hip flexibility Is mesmerizing.
jokes.setup: Discussing reasons to keep Buddy
jokes.punchline: Abed's analytical appreciation of Buddy's physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed analysis - technical appreciation of physical comedy mechanics

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Buddy's physical comedy is a masterclass in pratfall timing. The way he commits to each stumble—there's a geometric perfection to his falls. He's not trying to be funny; he's achieving comedy through pure kinetic geometry. It's like watching a Buster Keaton algorithm.

jokes.text: Your friend gary is so boring. - He was raised in finland. He doesn't get our humor.
jokes.setup: Pierce dismissing Gary
jokes.punchline: Shirley's defensive explanation makes Gary sound worse
jokes.explanation: Shirley's attempt to defend Gary actually reinforces why he'd be boring

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Gary, you're fired.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. He's not a bad guy. He just has a lot of personal problems. His wife left him, he's going through a custody battle, he's got a gambling addiction, and he may have committed a felony.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Shirley, that doesn't help.

jokes.text: He grew up in a land without sun!
jokes.setup: Continuing to defend Gary
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic description of Finland
jokes.explanation: Shirley's increasingly desperate defense of Gary becomes more dramatic

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Finland is a magical frozen wonderland where the Northern Lights dance across the sky like ethereal spirits, and the people are so pure-hearted that they've never even heard of crime!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: The lakes are so pristine they reflect the very soul of God, and the reindeer roam free in forests of ancient pine, untouched by the cruelty of the modern world!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: It's a place where sorrow itself is banished, where darkness cannot penetrate the eternal light of human goodness!

jokes.text: Holy crap. Wow. It says here some guy in seattle went nuts And killed his entire driver's ed class With a meat tenderizer.
jokes.setup: Group about to vote on Buddy
jokes.punchline: Annie's fake news story to influence vote
jokes.explanation: Specific absurd details (meat tenderizer, driver's ed) make the fake story funnier

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, before we vote, I have something important to share. I found this article about Buddy online.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: It says here that he's actually a former convict who ran an illegal pyramid scheme.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, where did you find this?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: The internet. It's definitely real. I definitely didn't make it up in the last five minutes.

jokes.text: Holy crap. Wow. It says here some guy in Seattle went nuts and killed his entire driver's ed class with a meat tenderizer.
jokes.setup: Jeff reading something shocking during voting discussion
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and violent fake news story
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that distracts from the vote. The specific details make it memorable dark comedy.

jokes.text: You lied about the guy in seattle With the meat tenderizer. You put your thumb on the scale. But you did it with a smile, And you did it so you could keep smiling. You're hawkeye.
jokes.setup: Jeff manipulating the vote against Buddy
jokes.punchline: Abed connecting Jeff's manipulation to his Hawkeye persona
jokes.explanation: Abed's insight connects Jeff's leadership style to the M.A.S.H. character arc

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You're doing the Hawkeye thing again.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What Hawkeye thing?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The thing where you manipulate everyone without them knowing it. It's your signature move. Clint Barton does it all the time in the comics. He's the only Avenger without superpowers, so he has to be smarter and sneakier than everyone else.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm nothing like Hawkeye.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You literally just did it to Buddy.

jokes.text: Racial profiling may not be right, But it can be economical.
jokes.setup: Dean trying to justify his text messaging decisions
jokes.punchline: Admits profiling is wrong but defends it for cost savings
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about institutionalized discrimination being treated as budget issue

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Look, I know profiling is wrong, but do you have any idea how much it costs to text message a minority?

jokes.text: Would you prefer 'imbecile' or 'incompetent?'
jokes.setup: Annie interviewing the Dean for her article
jokes.punchline: Offering choice between two insulting descriptions
jokes.explanation: Annie's newfound journalistic aggression giving Dean no good options

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Dean Pelton, for my article, I need you to choose: are you more of a spineless yes-man, or a craven boot-licker?

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Why do I have to choose? Can't I be both?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's... actually perfect for the article.

jokes.text: My best friend when I was six years old was a black man.
jokes.setup: Dean claiming he's not racist
jokes.punchline: Awkward childhood anecdote as racism defense
jokes.explanation: Classic 'I have a black friend' defense made more awkward by childhood context

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I'm not racist. I have a very diverse friend group. In fact, when I was a kid, my best friend was Black.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: His name was Reggie. We did everything together. We were inseparable.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Then one day he just... disappeared. Never saw him again.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: So you see? I'm not racist at all.

jokes.text: This is greendale, jeff. We can do whatever we want.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to convince Annie to drop the story
jokes.punchline: Annie throws Jeff's earlier words back at him
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Annie learned from Jeff's earlier manipulation

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, you need to drop this story. It's not worth it.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Not worth it? Jeff, you're the one who said 'A real journalist follows the truth wherever it leads, even if it destroys everything.' So which is it?

jokes.text: Was it the high kick? Was it 'you go, girl'?
jokes.setup: Buddy realizing he's been rejected
jokes.punchline: Lists his failed attempts to fit in
jokes.explanation: Sad callback to Buddy's earlier desperate attempts to impress

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: I tried joining the study group, they said no. I tried the paintball games, I got eliminated first. I even tried sitting at their table in the cafeteria and they moved.

jokes.text: I made you guys lemon squares with free-range eggs That I baked with my own hands.
jokes.setup: Buddy listing what he did for the group
jokes.punchline: Overly specific details about his baking efforts
jokes.explanation: Buddy's desperate attempt to prove his worth through baking details

dialogue.line: I need more context about the specific punchline text to accurately attribute speakers in this Community scene. The character "Buddy" is not a main character in Community, so either this may be a guest character or there may be a name variation. Could you provide the actual dialogue text from the punchline?

jokes.text: Maybe a little less sugar next time.
jokes.setup: Buddy's emotional plea about his lemon squares
jokes.punchline: Pierce's casual criticism of the recipe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - completely missing the emotional weight while offering cooking advice

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: These lemon squares represent years of my life, my heart, my soul poured into every bite.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, they're a little dry.

jokes.text: You're an uptight puppet master, And these are all your little puppets.
jokes.setup: Buddy having a breakdown
jokes.punchline: Accurately describes Jeff's group dynamics
jokes.explanation: Buddy's breakdown reveals an uncomfortable truth about Jeff's leadership

jokes.text: Make me...Hitler.
jokes.setup: Jeff telling Buddy to be reasonable and leave
jokes.punchline: Buddy's dramatic Nazi comparison
jokes.explanation: Buddy's complete breakdown into ridiculous historical comparison

jokes.text: Try getting that deal from hitler.
jokes.setup: Buddy comparing Jeff to Hitler
jokes.punchline: Jeff treating it as a reasonable negotiating comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - Jeff treating Hitler comparison as business negotiation

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: You're like Hitler.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, first of all, that's not a fair comparison. Second of all, what do you want?

jokes.text: [Buddy's Spanish outburst and physical meltdown]
jokes.setup: Buddy's complete breakdown in the library
jokes.punchline: Extended physical comedy meltdown with Spanish
jokes.explanation: Extended physical comedy sequence showing complete breakdown - commitment to bit

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: No, no, no, NO! This is not happening! I can't— I can't find the book! The book doesn't exist!

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: ¡Ay, Dios mío! ¡No puedo! ¡No puedo!

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: *collapses on floor, flailing* This is my life now! My life is OVER!

jokes.text: [Buddy has epic meltdown being dragged out of library with pants down screaming]
jokes.setup: Buddy refusing to leave the study group
jokes.punchline: Complete physical and emotional breakdown being forcibly removed
jokes.explanation: Major physical comedy sequence that brings Buddy's arc to a dramatic conclusion. The pants detail adds humiliation.

dialogue.speaker: Security Guard
dialogue.line: Sir, you need to leave the study room.

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: No! No, I can't leave! These are my friends!

dialogue.speaker: Security Guard
dialogue.line: I'm going to have to remove you.

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait—

jokes.text: If you'd ever actually seen the show, You'd know that hawkeye doesn't just bed nurses And drink martinis. He also had blood sprayed on his face And barked orders when the choppers came in. If he didn't, people died. He was a leader, jeff. That's your job.
jokes.setup: Jeff rejecting his Hawkeye comparison
jokes.punchline: Abed's serious analysis of what leadership actually means
jokes.explanation: Abed's deepest character moment - using pop culture to deliver real wisdom about leadership

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Leadership isn't about being the best archer or having the coolest outfit. It's about making decisions that affect people's lives, and being willing to live with the consequences of those decisions. Hawkeye doesn't have that burden because he's not real.

jokes.text: Because... You're jeff winger.
jokes.setup: Annie explaining why Jeff's anger mattered to her
jokes.punchline: Simple statement that Jeff is special/important
jokes.explanation: Annie's earnest hero worship of Jeff delivers emotional weight

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You're special to me, Jeff.

jokes.text: Ooh, you made me so happy I just peed a little.
jokes.setup: Jeff finally calling Abed 'Radar'
jokes.punchline: Abed's oversharing about his excitement
jokes.explanation: Abed's inappropriate sharing ruins the sweet moment

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You called me Radar. That's a reference to M*A*S*H. Radar O'Reilly was the company clerk who could predict things before they happened. I've been waiting for you to call me that since the pilot episode. I've mentally prepared a response. I was going to be cool about it, but I'm too excited. I can feel my pupils dilating. Is this what happiness feels like? I think I might cry. Not sad tears, but the kind of tears that come from the intersection of validation and pop culture recognition. I've watched M*A*S*H forty-seven times. Forty-seven. And now you've acknowledged that I'm like Radar. This is the best day of my life since the day you said 'Cool. Cool cool cool.'

jokes.text: Ooh, you made me so happy I just peed a little.
jokes.setup: Jeff calling him Radar (earning the nickname)
jokes.punchline: Abed's inappropriate physical reaction revelation
jokes.explanation: Decent Abed oversharing moment but feels a bit forced for the character

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Why do we call you Radar?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Because I have an uncanny ability to sense things before they happen.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's actually pretty cool.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, there's a downside. Every time I sense something bad is about to happen, I get an inappropriate physical reaction.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What kind of reaction?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I get an erection.

jokes.text: Annie, you flash your breasts as a decoy for the bum rush.
jokes.setup: Planning how to deal with stalking Buddy
jokes.punchline: Pierce's inappropriate tactical suggestion
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce inappropriate suggestion - character consistent but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What we need is a good old-fashioned drive-by. Nothing says 'back off' like a drive-by.

jokes.text: I didn't pick you as a decoy 'cause your breasts are so old.
jokes.setup: Shirley being offended she wasn't picked
jokes.punchline: Pierce's tactless explanation
jokes.explanation: Pierce's attempt at diplomacy making things worse - very in character

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Wait, why wasn't I picked?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, Shirley, you're a wonderful woman, but let's be honest—you'd probably try to bake the heist.

jokes.text: You guys were my safe date.
jokes.setup: Group welcoming Buddy back in
jokes.punchline: Buddy reveals he found something better
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal - group finally accepts Buddy but he's moved on

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Buddy! We've been looking everywhere for you!

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: Yeah, well I found something better.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Better than what? Us?

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: Better than Greendale. Better than all of you.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Dude, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Buddy
dialogue.line: I found a real community. One that doesn't fall apart every other week.

jokes.text: And that, ladies and gentlemen, Is why I voted to keep him.
jokes.setup: Buddy choosing other group over theirs
jokes.punchline: Jeff claiming this was his plan all along
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff move - taking credit for outcome he didn't plan, perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, so he chose them over us. That's fine. That was actually part of my plan.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I wanted him to think he had a choice.

tmdb_overview: Jeff accepts an invitation to be the editor of the school paper as he and his friends fend off an interloper who wants to join their study group.
insight: Solid character comedy carries a mid-tier episode through 70 jokes in 23 minutes.
